verdict: customize.toml is well-structured for a memory agent: metadata-only, narrow org hooks, sanctum correctly identified as primary surface. One secondary config mechanism found.

findings.title: Secondary config mechanism reads config.yaml/config.user.yaml in init-sanctum.py
findings.evidence: init-sanctum.py reads _bmad/config.yaml and _bmad/config.user.yaml for user_name (default: friend) and communication_language (default: English), creating a parallel customization surface outside the customize.toml contract. These files are read but don't exist in this project, so defaults always apply—making the mechanism both a proliferation concern and effectively unreachable.
findings.recommendation: Remove the config.yaml/config.user.yaml codepath from init-sanctum.py. For a memory agent, user_name and communication_language should either be set conversationally during First Breath or, if they must be build-time configurable, added as bona-fide fields in customize.toml's agent-specific configurables section.
